steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence states that “fragmentation inevitably ______ alterations to local ecological processes.” The blank needs a verb meaning that fragmentation causes or produces those alterations — not one that prevents, absorbs, or reduces them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'engenders': Means to bring about or give rise to. Fragmentation engenders (produces) alterations — exactly the causal relationship the sentence requires. ✓
  • Choice B — 'preempts': Means to prevent or forestall something before it occurs. This would say fragmentation prevents alterations, which contradicts the sentence's meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — 'subsumes': Means to include or absorb something into a larger category. Fragmentation cannot categorize alterations; this makes no logical sense here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'attenuates': Means to weaken or reduce. This would say fragmentation weakens alterations, contradicting the idea that the changes have a significant impact on specialist species. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
